name: Two Pointers
description: Uses two references pointing to different indices/elements to process data, reducing time complexity from O(N^2) to O(N).
key_identifiers: ["Sorted arrays or lists","Finding pairs that satisfy a condition","Reversing elements or checking symmetry"]
common_problems: ["Two Sum II (Sorted)","Valid Palindrome","Container With Most Water","3Sum"]

name: Sliding Window
description: Maintains a subset of contiguous elements (window) that expands or shrinks dynamically, avoiding redundant calculations over sub-arrays.
key_identifiers: ["Subarrays or substrings with target constraints","Finding maximum/minimum sum, length, or distinct elements","Linear time complexity requirements"]
common_problems: ["Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K","Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters","Minimum Size Subarray Sum","Permutation in String"]

name: Fast & Slow Pointers
description: Uses two pointers moving at different speeds (usually slow moves 1 step, fast moves 2 steps) to detect cycles or find midpoints.
key_identifiers: ["Singly linked lists with loops","Finding midpoints of sequences","Finding cyclic dependencies or happy numbers"]
common_problems: ["Linked List Cycle","Middle of the Linked List","Happy Number","Palindrome Linked List"]

name: Merge Intervals
description: Handles overlapping ranges or intervals by sorting them by start time and merging overlapping boundaries.
key_identifiers: ["Ranges or schedules given as pairs","Finding scheduling overlaps or overlaps in time","Inserting new schedules into sorted timelines"]

name: Topological Sort
description: Finds a linear ordering of directed acyclic graph (DAG) nodes such that for every directed edge U -> V, node U comes before V.
key_identifiers: ["Prerequisites or order of tasks","Directed graphs without cycles","Sequencing multiple dependencies"]
common_problems: ["Course Schedule","Course Schedule II","Minimum Height Trees","Alien Dictionary"]
